claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 20 years old and lives with me at home. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current situation since he's 20 years old.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he enrolled in a course at school, college, or university, or participating in approved training?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school a while back and isn't studying anywhere right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have one more important question:

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work. He's not in employment at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one final question:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit, Employment and Support Allowance, or any other benefits?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Assessment Result

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are **under 20 years old AND in approved education or training**.

Since Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria. The maximum age for Child Benefit is under 20, and that only applies when the young person is still in approved education or training.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future (though given his age, he would need to be under 20 to qualify), you would need to reassess eligibility at that time.

Is there anything else I can help clarify about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and is NOT currently in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria. The age limit for Child Benefit is under 20 years old for those in approved education or training, and Alex has already turned 20 and is not in such education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old. Alex lives at home but is not in employment, education, or training, and does not receive any benefits. Child Benefit eligibility extends to children aged 16-19 only if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, he exceeds the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and is NOT currently in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria. The age limit for Child Benefit is under 20 years old for those in approved education or training, and Alex has already turned 20 and is not in such education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old. Alex lives at home but is not in employment, education, or training, and does not receive any benefits. Child Benefit eligibility extends to children aged 16-19 only if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, he exceeds the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility.
